seoTitle: How to Delete a Microsoft Loop Workspace (2026)
metaDescription: Permanently remove an outdated Microsoft Loop workspace from your dashboard, including how to handle admin-locked or cached directory errors.
intro: Dead project boards left sitting in your dashboard can clutter your organization and eat into storage limits. This guide covers permanently deleting a Microsoft Loop workspace from the home dashboard, what the confirmation warning means, and what to do if a deletion doesn't seem to take.

steps.heading: Open your dashboard
steps.body: Go to loop.cloud.microsoft and click the top left Loop logo to access your master workspace directory.

steps.heading: Find the workspace to remove
steps.body: Move your cursor over the target workspace tile inside your dashboard view.

steps.heading: Open the options menu
steps.body: Click the three dots icon that appears on the tile to open the context menu drawer.

steps.heading: Select Delete
steps.body: Choose the Delete command from the dropdown to trigger the confirmation warning box.

steps.heading: Confirm the deletion
steps.body: Double-check your project assets, then click the red Delete button to permanently erase the workspace.

steps.heading: Refresh if the tile is still showing
steps.body: If the deleted workspace still appears due to caching, run a quick page refresh to update your dashboard grid.

faq.question: Is deleting a workspace reversible?
faq.answer: Deleting a workspace is a permanent action, so it's important to review its contents before confirming.

faq.question: Why can't I delete a shared team workspace?
faq.answer: Team-shared or corporate tenant-locked directories often require admin approval before they can be permanently erased.

faq.question: Why does the workspace still show up after I deleted it?
faq.answer: This is usually a caching issue — refreshing the page typically clears it from the dashboard view.

faq.question: Does deleting a workspace free up storage space?
faq.answer: Yes, cleaning up orphaned workspaces helps prevent running into personal account storage caps.
